resources.string: Location and Wi-Fi permissions are required to scan for exact network names (SSID) and perform full signal level diagnostics.
Permissions are disabled. Please enable Location and Wi-Fi permissions in App Settings.
SSID — The service set identifier is the public name of your wireless network.
Frequency Band — 2.4 GHz offers longer range but slower speeds, while 5 GHz offers higher speeds but shorter range.
Link Speed — The maximum theoretical rate at which data can be transferred between your device and the Wi-Fi router.
Active Speed — Calculated in real-time by analyzing the delta of bytes received (↓) and transmitted (↑) over the network interfaces.
Not connected to any wireless network. Please connect to Wi-Fi or Cellular network to start diagnostics.
Your device does not have the following sensors:
Measures the acceleration force applied to the device on all three physical axes (x, y, and z), including gravity.
Measures the rate of rotation around the x, y, and z axes. It detects how fast the device is spinning.
Measures the ambient magnetic field in the x, y, and z axes. It\'s commonly used as a digital compass.
Measures the distance of an object from the front of the device. Most sensors return only \'near\' or \'far\' values.
Represents the orientation of the device as a combination of an axis and an angle. It\'s more stable than using accelerometer alone.
Measures atmospheric pressure. It\'s used to determine altitude and can help predict weather changes.
Measures the ambient (environmental) air temperature around the device.
Measures the relative humidity of the surrounding air as a percentage.
Measures the acceleration force applied to the device without gravity. Shows the pure motion of the device.
Uses a micro-electromechanical system (MEMS) with a proof mass that deflects when acceleration is applied. The deflection is measured electrically to determine acceleration.
Uses the Coriolis effect on a vibrating mass. When the device rotates, the vibration pattern changes, and this change is measured to calculate rotational velocity.
Uses a Hall effect sensor or magnetoresistive material that changes its electrical resistance when exposed to a magnetic field.
Uses an infrared LED that emits light and a receiver that detects reflections. The time or intensity of the reflection indicates distance.
Fuses data from accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer using sensor fusion algorithms to provide accurate device orientation in 3D space.
Uses a MEMS piezoresistive sensor with a membrane that deflects with pressure changes. The deflection changes resistance, which is measured electrically.
Uses a thermistor or RTD (resistance temperature detector) that changes resistance based on temperature. Not available on most smartphones.
Uses a capacitive sensor with a moisture-absorbing dielectric. Capacitance changes with humidity levels, which is measured electrically.
Same MEMS technology as the accelerometer, but the system automatically subtracts the gravitational component (9.81 m/s²) to show only linear motion.
The magnetometer requires dedicated hardware (compass). Many modern mid-range/high-end devices don\'t include it to reduce costs or internal space.
The proximity sensor uses an infrared LED and detector. Some devices use alternative (software) solutions or don\'t include it.
The rotation vector combines accelerometer, gyroscope, and magnetometer. If any are missing, this sensor won\'t be available.
The barometer (pressure sensor) is rare in smartphones. It\'s mainly found in high-end devices or tablets designed for professional use.
This sensor measures the actual ambient temperature of the environment. Most smartphones don\'t include it because they use battery/processor temperature sensors.
The relative humidity sensor is generally only in rugged devices or high-end devices focused on health/adventure.
Similar to the accelerometer but without gravity. If the base accelerometer isn\'t available, this won\'t be either.
This sensor is not available on your device.

This section displays all available sensors on your device, including accelerometers, gyroscopes, magnetometers, and more. Tap any sensor to view detailed information about its vendor, power consumption, and technical specifications.
Enable dark theme for the application
Show more decimal places for sensor values
Display sensor units next to values
Are you sure you want to reset the dashboard?
Override System Monitor provides real-time monitoring of your device\'s sensors, network, battery, and system information.
